channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo Appeals for Calm After Vatican Meeting With Rubio
item.description: Pope Leo asked God to inspire world leaders to reduce tensions and hatred in an address marking his first anniversary as head of the Catholic Church, following his meeting with U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio amid strained U.S.-Holy See relations.

item.title: 🇮🇹 Rubio Questions Allied Support on Iran After Italy Talks
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio met Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni and questioned why allies including Italy were not backing U.S. efforts to confront Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Markets Understate Political Dysfunction After Reform Surge
item.description: British bond yields remained calm after Reform UK gains in local elections, but the rise of fringe parties bodes ill for economic policy coherence, with upstarts less likely than current government to deliver coherent policy.

item.title: 🇮🇳 India Inflation Likely Rose to 3.8% as Fuel Costs Climb
item.description: India annual consumer inflation likely moved closer to the central bank 4% target in April as higher fuel costs from the U.S.-Iran conflict began feeding into prices, according to a Reuters poll of economists.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia and Ukraine agree to three-day ceasefire with prisoner swap
item.description: Russia and Ukraine confirmed a US-brokered three-day ceasefire from May 9-11, with a planned 1,000-for-1,000 prisoner swap. Trump expressed hope for an extension.

item.title: 🛡️ Ukraine running short of air defence missiles after Russian winter campaign
item.description: Ukraine's air force warned it is running short of air defence missiles after Russia's massive winter attack campaign, as it braces for further strikes.

item.title: 🇹🇼 US concerned Taiwan defense delays are "concession" to China
item.description: The US State Department warned that further delays to Taiwan's military spending represent a 'concession' to China, as Taipei's defense ministry detailed the impact of projects excluded from a budget package.

item.title: 🇵🇾 Paraguay president affirms strong Taiwan ties during Taipei visit
item.description: Paraguay President Santiago Pena said his country "deeply values" relations with Taiwan and will continue to offer support, during a formal welcome in Taipei.

item.title: 🇬🇧 British MPs to visit China for first time in seven years
item.description: A cross-party delegation of British lawmakers will visit China this month in a sign of warming ties after Beijing agreed to lift sanctions on six MPs following Prime Minister Keir Starmer's January visit.

item.title: 🇫🇷 France arrests Ukrainian linked to notorious Russian torture prison in occupied Donetsk
item.description: French authorities detained a suspect accused of assisting the head of the Izolyatsia prison camp in torturing inmates, marking the second time a foreign country has detained a suspect charged with war crimes in Ukraine.

item.title: 🔥 Massive forest fire sparked by Russian strikes in Ukraine doubles in size to 4,300 hectares
item.description: The forest fire near Ukraine's border with Russia has expanded from 2,400 to 4,300 hectares (10,600 acres), with FPV drones reportedly blocking firefighter access to the area.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU prepares to back Belgium in frozen assets fight against Russian oligarchs
item.description: Russian oligarchs are using a 1989 Soviet-era investment treaty to challenge frozen asset seizures, prompting Belgium to seek EU support in the legal battle over sanctions enforcement.

item.title: 🇦🇸 ASEAN leaders agree to create shared fuel reserve for future crises
item.description: Southeast Asian nations are establishing a regional fuel stockpile to strengthen energy security and resilience against supply disruptions, amid growing concerns about global instability.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US Sanctions Chinese Firms for Aiding Iran Drone Program
item.description: The US Treasury sanctioned 10 individuals and companies from China and Hong Kong for supplying materials for Iran's Shahed drones and ballistic missiles, escalating pressure on Tehran's weapons supply chain.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚓ CIA Report: Iran Could Withstand Hormuz Blockade for Four Months
item.description: A US intelligence analysis concludes Tehran has stockpiles to survive a naval blockade for months, as fighting flares in the Gulf despite ongoing ceasefire negotiations.

item.title: 🇺🇳 US Revises UN Iran Resolution, China and Russia Still Expected to Veto
item.description: Washington revised its proposed UN resolution demanding Iran halt attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, but diplomats say the changes are unlikely to prevent Chinese and Russian vetoes.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan Approves $25 Billion Defense Boost, Less Than Government Sought
item.description: Taiwan's opposition-controlled parliament approved extra defense spending of $25 billion, about two-thirds of what the government requested to counter China's military modernization.

item.title: 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿 Pro-Independence Parties Surge Across Britain in Historic Election Shift
item.description: Three of the United Kingdom's four nations are now set to be governed by pro-independence parties after elections that nationalists say mark a turning point for the centuries-old union.

item.title: 🏛️ Ex-NATO Chief Warns of Alliance Disintegration
item.description: Former NATO Secretary General Anders Fogh Rasmussen warned of potential alliance disintegration as European countries increasingly take leadership roles while the US role recedes under Trump administration policies.

item.title: 🛢️⚔️ US Strikes Two Iranian-Flagged Oil Tankers in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US Central Command confirmed strikes on two empty Iranian oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz as tensions escalate despite ongoing ceasefire negotiations. The incident marks another challenge to the fragile truce.

item.title: 🇮🇶⚔️ Foreign Militias Deployed in Iran to Enforce Regime Crackdown
item.description: Iraqi Hashd al-Shaabi militias are now patrolling Iranian streets as the regime intensifies its internal crackdown, with residents saying it feels like an occupation.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸 Iran Questions US Commitment to Diplomacy After Naval Escalation
item.description: Foreign Minister Araghchi told his Turkish counterpart that US naval escalation in the Strait of Hormuz raises doubts about Washingtons commitment to ongoing peace talks.

item.title: 🇭🇹⚖️ Four Convicted in US Trial Over Haiti Presidential Assassination
item.description: A US jury convicted four people of conspiracy in connection with the 2021 assassination of Haitian President Jovenel Moïse, with prosecutors saying the conspirators hoped to enrich themselves.

item.title: 🇮🇷🇺🇸 Iran accuses US of reckless military adventure as Hormuz clashes continue
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi accused the US of opting for military action whenever diplomacy is on the table, as both sides exchanged attacks in the Strait of Hormuz and the US enforced a naval blockade of Iranian ports.

item.title: 💻 Major cyber attack disrupts thousands of universities and schools worldwide
item.description: Hacking group ShinyHunters claimed responsibility for an attack on Canvas academic software, affecting approximately 9,000 institutions globally. Students faced exam cancellations and coursework disruptions across US, Canada, Australia and Europe.
